description_long: This is a classic wood-handled axe featuring a robust, slightly curved handle made from textured, natural wood. The head of the axe is forged from metal, with a broad, wedge-shaped blade on one side and a flat poll on the opposite end. The blade is wide and slightly convex, designed for efficient chopping, and the metal head is securely mounted onto the handle. The wood grain is visible along the handle, giving it a rustic and utilitarian appearance. The metal head has a matte, slightly worn finish, indicating practical use rather than decorative intent. This tool is typical of those found in urban garages, maintenance sheds, or park service areas.
description: A traditional wood-handled axe with a broad metal blade and a slightly curved, textured wooden handle. The metal head is securely mounted and shows a matte, utilitarian finish.
description_view_0: The axe is seen from the top, showing the narrow profile of the blade and the rounded end of the handle.
description_view_1: The axe is viewed from the side, clearly displaying the full length of the wooden handle and the wide, wedge-shaped metal blade.
description_view_2: The axe is seen from the bottom, highlighting the back of the metal head and the straight alignment of the handle.
description_view_3: The opposite side view of the axe, showing the blade's edge and the handle's curvature from the other direction.
attribute_handle_type: Slightly curved, cylindrical wooden handle with visible grain, textured for grip, oval cross-section.
attribute_blade_design: Single broad wedge-shaped steel blade with a convex edge and a flat poll; no beard or decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the blade head fitted onto the handle.
